description: Indian listed companies that benefit directly or indirectly from global AI adoption through AI server/HPC manufacturing, optical networking & fibre connectivity, data-centre power equipment (transformers/switchgear), electronics manufacturing (EMS) for AI hardware, and cables/wiring for data-centre buildouts. General IT services companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCLTech, etc.) are intentionally excluded per the brief, even though several have announced their own AI data-centre capex (e.g. HCLTech's ~Rs 3,500 crore AI data-centre investment announced 13Jul26) - these are noted only as demand-side context, not as covered companies.
data_quality_note: Prices, order-win values and financial figures below are sourced from market reports through 21Jul26 and are as accurate as available search data allows. Figures marked 'approx'/'estimated' are directional. This is not investment advice - please verify against a live terminal before acting.

Netweb Technologies India.category: AI Servers / HPC Systems (India's only listed pure-play AI infrastructure company)
Netweb Technologies India.ai_link: Designs, manufactures and sells AI servers, supercomputers, private/hybrid cloud (HCI), high-performance storage and networking under its 'Tyrone' brand; builds NVIDIA Blackwell-based GPU-accelerated systems for government, defence, BFSI and enterprise AI workloads.
Netweb Technologies India.fy26_financials: Revenue Rs 2,184 crore (+90% YoY), PAT Rs 206 crore (+81% YoY), ROE ~28.5-32.8%, ROCE ~37.5%
current_global_impact: Direct beneficiary of India's Sovereign AI push under the IndiaAI Mission - secured a Rs 1,734 crore order for GPU-accelerated Blackwell-architecture systems
Launched 'Tyrone Camarero GB200' AI supercomputer and 'Tyrone Camarero Spark' personal AI computer, riding global GPU/AI hardware upcycle
Stock rallied ~10-19% in a two-day move on AI/data-centre collaboration news (incl. tie-up referencing Vertiv) and on strong Nvidia quarterly results given its GPU-system partnership
India HPC market estimated at ~$643.6 billion (FY25) growing toward ~$918.6 billion by FY29 (~9.3% CAGR), a direct addressable market
markets_served: India (primary - govt, defence, BFSI, enterprise, education/research)
Limited direct export exposure vs. peers below; largely a domestic AI-compute play
expected_future_impact: Order pipeline in HPC/AI workloads should keep growing with IndiaAI Mission-linked and enterprise GPU-cluster demand
Analyst 12-month base case target ~Rs 4,800 (bull case ~Rs 6,000), 3-year base case ~Rs 8,500 per one broker note (May 2026) - illustrates high growth expectations already priced in
Key risks: dependence on large government/defence order timing, high P/E (~120x+), reliance on external borrowing for working capital

Sterlite Technologies (STL).category: Optical Fibre & Fibre Cables / Data-Centre Connectivity
Sterlite Technologies (STL).ai_link: Largest optical fibre and optical fibre cable (OFC) manufacturer in India (~8% global ex-China OFC market share, FY26); supplies high-capacity fibre and optical connectivity products that link GPU servers, storage and switches inside AI/hyperscale data centres.
current_global_impact: Won a multi-year Product Award Letter (PAL) worth more than $1 billion (reports also cite ~$1.1bn) to supply optical connectivity products to a global hyperscaler for AI data-centre build-out
Stock has been one of the standout AI-infrastructure movers on Indian markets: up ~357% over 1 year and ~449% over 6 months (as of 21Jul26)
Raised Rs 1,500 crore via QIP and board approved raising up to Rs 2,000 crore in fresh capital to fund expansion/reduce debt
Won a European patent dispute against Japan's Fujikura, strengthening its IP position in the UK/Europe optical connectivity market
Partners with global telcos/hyperscalers including British Telecom, Vocus (Australia), du Telecom, Netomnia and Connexin (Europe/UK/APAC)
expected_future_impact: Execution of the ~$1bn+ hyperscaler AI data-centre order over coming quarters is the single biggest near-term re-rating catalyst

Global preform supply (60-70% of fibre manufacturing cost) remains concentrated in China with 2-3 year lead times for new capacity - a potential structural tailwind for STL if it can add backward-integrated capacity
Q1 FY27 results due 24Jul26; trading window was closed 1-26Jul26 ahead of results
Key risk: after an extremely sharp run-up (300%+ in a year), stock has already corrected ~12-22% from its June 2026 high of Rs 651.90-679.90, showing high volatility

HFCL Limited.category: Optical Fibre Cables, Telecom Equipment & Data-Centre Connectivity
HFCL Limited.ai_link: Manufactures optical fibre cables and telecom/networking equipment; has pivoted a growing share of its business toward AI data-centre connectivity through its new 'OptiQ AI' optical connectivity brand.
HFCL Limited.fy26_financials: Consolidated revenue Rs 4,949 crore (nearly doubled YoY), EBITDA ~Rs 827 crore, PAT ~Rs 329 crore
current_global_impact: Secured one of its largest-ever contracts: an international hyperscaler order worth approx Rs 10,159 crore for optical fibre cables, to be executed over five years
Won a separate ~$52 million (~Rs 496 crore) export order for optical fibre-based data-centre connectivity solutions from an overseas hyperscale customer (announced 10Jul26, execution by Dec 2026)
Total order book has crossed Rs 21,000 crore - larger than the company's own market capitalisation
Stock has rallied nearly 200% over the past six months on data-centre/AI capex re-rating
Credit rating upgraded by CARE Ratings (1Jul26), reflecting improved credit profile amid the order-book surge
markets_served: India (BharatNet Phase-III telecom rollout, defence communications)
expected_future_impact: Revenue visibility strong given order book >4x FY26 revenue, but recognition will be staggered over multi-year execution (hyperscaler order through ~2031, export order through Dec 2026)
Further AI data-centre and hyperscaler order wins expected to be the key re-rating trigger going into Q1 FY27 results
Preform manufacturing facility investment (~Rs 580 crore, arm HFCL Technologies) aims to backward-integrate and reduce China dependency for fibre raw material
Risk: muted single-day stock reaction to large order announcements suggests the market may already be pricing in continued order wins; execution/margin delivery is now the key watch item

CG Power and Industrial Solutions.ai_link: Manufactures power/distribution transformers, switchgear and substation equipment - critical electrical infrastructure needed to power AI/hyperscale data centres, which require far higher and steadier electrical loads than conventional facilities.
current_global_impact: Secured a large-scale US data-centre project order worth Rs 900 crore for supply of power transformers (disclosed ~early Feb 2026)

Shares surged as much as 10% intraday in Feb 2026 on the order news combined with the India-US trade deal (US tariffs on Indian goods cut from 50% to 18%), which markets read as a broad positive for India's electronics/semiconductor/transformer export ecosystem
expected_future_impact: Global data-centre power demand is a structural tailwind: US DOE projects data centres could account for up to 12% of US electricity demand by 2028, driving sustained transformer/switchgear order flow
Further large overseas data-centre orders are a plausible re-rating catalyst given the Rs 900 crore US order already won

Peer/comparable global names (Eaton, etc.) are seeing record backlogs in power-generation/transformer segments for data centres, suggesting the addressable opportunity for CG Power is expanding globally, not just in India

Kaynes Technology India.ai_link: Integrated electronics manufacturer (design, PCB assembly, box-build) serving industrial, IoT, automotive and increasingly AI-hardware-adjacent electronics; also has semiconductor OSAT ambitions (Kaynes Semicon) relevant to the AI hardware supply chain.
current_global_impact: Stock has fallen sharply - down ~43% over the past year and ~6% over six months (as of 21Jul26) - after a 52% correction from its 7Jul2025 high of Rs 7,705, driven by weak Q4 FY26 results that missed expectations
Rallied ~5% in Feb 2026 alongside Dixon/CG Power on the India-US trade deal tariff cut, seen as positive for India's electronics/semiconductor ecosystem
Q3 FY26 (Dec 2025 quarter) sales grew 21.6% YoY to Rs 804 crore, showing underlying growth even as the stock de-rated on valuation/guidance concerns
markets_served: India (industrial, auto, defence, medical, IT hardware)
expected_future_impact: Semiconductor OSAT (outsourced assembly & test) buildout under India's semiconductor mission is a multi-year growth driver if execution stays on track
Elevated valuation (P/E ~61-88x) means the stock remains highly sensitive to any earnings disappointment, as seen in the recent 52% drawdown
Broader EMS sector seen as a beneficiary of India's PLI scheme and global supply-chain diversification away from China, which supports the medium-term thesis despite near-term stock weakness

Dixon Technologies (India).category: Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) - includes servers & networking hardware
Dixon Technologies (India).ai_link: India's largest EMS company; manufactures servers, networking equipment, and other electronic hardware (alongside its larger mobile-phone/consumer-electronics business) that feeds into AI/data-centre infrastructure supply chains.
current_global_impact: Stock has corrected sharply from its 52-week/all-time high of ~Rs 18,471-18,549 (hit 7Jan2026) to the ~Rs 10,700-11,000 range - a drawdown of roughly 38-42%
Correction driven by concerns over pending PN3 regulatory approvals for its JVs with Vivo and HKC, and a slowdown in budget-smartphone volumes (FY27E volume estimates cut from 63mn to 60mn units)
Rallied ~6.4% in Feb 2026 alongside Kaynes/CG Power on the India-US tariff-cut trade deal news
Broader EMS sector (Dixon, Kaynes, Cyient DLM) surged on a ~Rs 1.9 lakh crore government semiconductor/mobile-manufacturing incentive push
markets_served: Export potential via global electronics supply-chain diversification from China
expected_future_impact: Resolution of pending JV regulatory approvals (Vivo, HKC) is a key near-term catalyst/risk
Government's PLI and semiconductor/electronics manufacturing incentives remain a structural tailwind for AI-hardware-adjacent server/networking manufacturing volumes
Very high valuation (P/E >117x) means continued sensitivity to any volume or margin disappointment

Voltamp Transformers.ai_link: Manufactures power, distribution and dry-type transformers; part of the broader transformer/switchgear ecosystem that data centres (including AI facilities) depend on for stable high-capacity power supply, alongside its core industrial/utility customer base.
current_global_impact: Stock up ~37.9% over the past 6 months but roughly flat to slightly down (~-0.13% to -0.27%) over the past 1 year, reflecting a sharp recovery from a weak patch in late 2025/early 2026
Q4 FY26 results were weak - profit fell ~50.5% YoY and shares plunged ~20% on the day, hit by copper/aluminium input cost pressure
FY26 revenue grew 11% to Rs 2,153.7 crore even as profitability was squeezed
markets_served: Primarily India (utilities, industrial, infrastructure); limited direct global data-centre exposure disclosed versus HFCL/STL/CG Power
expected_future_impact: India's distribution transformer sector is expanding an estimated 12-18% annually on urbanisation, renewable integration and government infra capex (Union Budget 2026-27 earmarked Rs 11.21 lakh crore for infrastructure)
Commodity (copper/aluminium) price volatility is a key margin risk highlighted by the company's own Q4 results
Analyst 12-month price targets of Rs 8,900-10,200 (as of April 2026) implied 19-36% upside from the then-CMP of Rs 7,500 - stock has since moved through much of that range

Transformers and Rectifiers (India).ai_link: Manufactures power transformers and is developing HVDC (high-voltage direct current) capability - relevant to large-load, high-reliability power infrastructure of the type large data centres require.
current_global_impact: FY26 annual revenue grew 23-24% YoY to Rs 2,395 crore; Q4 FY26 PAT of Rs 89 crore
Order book stands at Rs 5,005 crore, including an HVDC milestone win, indicating diversification into higher-value grid infrastructure
expected_future_impact: HVDC and high-capacity transformer capability positions the company to participate in grid-strengthening projects that large data-centre clusters typically require
Sector-wide transformer demand tailwind from India's infrastructure capex and renewable energy integration

Polycab India.ai_link: India's largest wires and cables manufacturer; supplies power and industrial cabling that forms part of the electrical backbone of data-centre facilities, alongside its larger core building-wire and FMEG business.
current_global_impact: Q3 FY26 revenue grew 46.1% YoY to Rs 7,636 crore; net profit grew 35.8% YoY to Rs 630 crore, reflecting strong underlying demand across its cable and FMEG portfolio

Identified by market commentary as one of several cable stocks (alongside KEI Industries) positioned to benefit from India's data-centre capex boom, given cables/electrical infrastructure are a core requirement for any data-centre buildout
Stock hit a fresh all-time high of Rs 7,947.35 on 8Jan26 before correcting
markets_served: Primarily India (residential, commercial, industrial); growing exports
expected_future_impact: Data-centre capex boom is one of several demand drivers (alongside real estate, renewables, and general infrastructure capex) rather than the company's dominant revenue driver - so AI/data-centre exposure is indirect and diluted versus pure-plays like STL/HFCL

Broader read-through: as India's data-centre IT load has grown from ~350MW (2019) to ~1.5-1.6GW (2025), a ~29% CAGR per Nomura/Bloomberg data cited in market reports, cabling demand from this segment should keep scaling

Syrma SGS Technology.ai_link: Chennai-based EMS provider offering design, PCB assembly and box-build manufacturing across industrial, automotive, consumer, healthcare and IT-hardware customers; a growing share of its industrial/IT segment feeds into servers, networking and electronics used in AI/data-centre hardware.
Syrma SGS Technology.fy26_financials: Revenue Rs 4,819 crore, Profit Rs 346 crore, ROE ~10.6% (3-yr avg)
current_global_impact: Stock up ~99% over 1 year and ~96% over 6 months (as of 20Jul26), among the strongest EMS re-ratings in the sector
Hit a fresh 52-week high after announcing a new PCB manufacturing facility in Andhra Pradesh (Jul 2025), expanding capacity relevant to electronics/server-board manufacturing
New JV with Kaga Electronics India (~Rs 15 crore investment, Jun 2026) to set up an additional EMS facility, broadening manufacturing scale
New CEO Jaidit Singh Brar took charge (29 Jun 2026), signalling a management transition during a period of rapid growth
markets_served: Global and domestic OEM customers across industrial, auto, consumer and healthcare electronics
expected_future_impact: Continued capacity expansion (new PCB and EMS facilities) should support further volume growth as India's electronics manufacturing and supply-chain-diversification story continues

Relatively low ROE (~10.6%) versus its very high valuation (P/E ~89x) is a key risk flagged by fundamentals-focused analysts - the AI/data-centre narrative is a smaller part of a broader diversified EMS business versus pure-plays like Netweb

Tejas Networks.ai_link: Designs and manufactures optical transport, fibre broadband access, switching/routing and 4G/5G networking products - the networking layer that connects data-centre and telecom infrastructure, relevant to AI-era bandwidth demand even though the company is not an AI-specific pure-play.
current_global_impact: Launched its next-generation optical transport platform, the TJ1600-D3, at a global industry event, with shares surging over 8% on the launch
Signed an agreement with Japan's NEC Corporation to manufacture and supply 5G massive MIMO products, broadening its export/partnership base
Reported mixed Q4 FY26 results with rising losses even as revenue grew, and shares fell over 5% on the print - highlighting execution/profitability challenges despite order momentum
Backed by the Tata Group, which has strengthened its balance sheet and execution credibility
Stock has been highly volatile - rallying ~55% in a single week at one point in the year, then correcting sharply after weak quarterly numbers
markets_served: Export markets across Southeast Asia, Africa and other emerging markets (~75 countries served)
expected_future_impact: Rapid rollout of 5G, data centres and fibre networks in India and emerging markets provides long-term growth visibility, per sell-side commentary
Government support for indigenous telecom manufacturing (Make in India) and rising export opportunities are structural tailwinds
Key risk: persistent losses and volatile quarterly execution mean the stock remains sentiment-driven around order announcements rather than steady earnings delivery

KEI Industries.ai_link: Major Indian wire and cable manufacturer supplying low/high-tension cables and EPC solutions; identified alongside Polycab as a beneficiary of India's data-centre capex boom given cabling's role in every data-centre buildout.
KEI Industries.fy26_financials: Revenue Rs 11,748 crore, Profit Rs 918 crore
current_global_impact: Stock up ~29% over 1 year and ~14.1% over 6 months (as of 20Jul26)
Credit ratings reaffirmed at CARE AA+ (long-term)/CARE A1+ (short-term), June 2026, reflecting a stable credit profile amid capacity expansion
Exports to over 50 countries via its Institutional and Export business segments, in addition to strong Retail cable demand domestically
expected_future_impact: Data-centre cabling demand is one of several tailwinds (alongside real estate, renewables and general infra capex) rather than a dedicated data-centre revenue line - exposure is indirect, similar to Polycab
Continued capacity expansion and a strong balance sheet position KEI to capture incremental cabling demand as India's data-centre IT load keeps scaling

Finolex Cables.ai_link: Established Indian cable manufacturer supplying electrical and communication cables; general electrical-infrastructure exposure to the data-centre buildout theme via cabling demand, though with less explicit data-centre-specific order disclosure than KEI/Polycab/HFCL.
current_global_impact: Trades as part of the broader listed cable basket (alongside Polycab, KEI, RR Kabel) that market commentary flags as beneficiaries of India's data-centre and infrastructure capex cycle
expected_future_impact: Any pickup in electrical/communication cabling demand tied to India's data-centre and broader infrastructure buildout would be a incremental (not primary) tailwind for the stock, similar to other diversified cable makers

ABB India.ai_link: Subsidiary of Swiss-Swedish ABB Ltd; its Electrification segment supplies power distribution, switchgear and automation equipment directly to hyperscaler and data-centre build-outs, alongside industrial, renewables and EV-charging infrastructure.
current_global_impact: Data centres now account for approx. 11% of ABB India's total order book, per company disclosures
Record order backlog of Rs 11,094 crore (Q1 CY26), with total order intake up 25% YoY

Electrification segment order intake grew 36% YoY - the strongest of any segment - with an order backlog of ~Rs 4,000 crore (~36% of total backlog), driven by data-centre automation and power distribution for hyperscaler build-outs, renewables integration, grid upgrades (National Electricity Plan) and EV charging
Electrification Q1 CY26 revenue Rs 1,564 crore (+15% YoY), PBIT Rs 237 crore, at >15% PBIT margin; segment is 49% of continuing-ops revenue mix

ABB Group announced a $75 million India capex commitment (March 2026) across five locations, including domestic data-centre automation capability, expanded HV motor manufacturing (Bengaluru), EV charging and transformer capacity, fully funded from ABB India's Rs 6,042 crore net cash
Leadership transition: T K Sridhar to take over as Managing Director from January 2027, with Sanjeev Sharma moving to the Board
markets_served: Parent ABB Ltd serves global hyperscaler/data-centre customers, with India increasingly positioned as a manufacturing and R&D anchor rather than just a cost hub
expected_future_impact: Data-centre automation and power distribution is explicitly called out by management as one of the strongest demand drivers into CY26-27, alongside renewables and grid-upgrade capex
Strong net-cash balance sheet (Rs 6,042 crore) gives room for continued India-specific capacity investment without leverage risk

As one of the few India-listed entities with explicit, quantified data-centre order-book disclosure (~11% of backlog), ABB India offers relatively higher-confidence AI-infrastructure exposure versus story-stock peers

Schneider Electric Infrastructure.ai_link: Indian listed arm of Schneider Electric focused on electricity distribution products and systems; positioned by analysts around 'grid modernization and data centre demand' as a long-term growth driver, given data centres require robust, modernized power distribution.
Schneider Electric Infrastructure.current_price_inr_approx: 906 (recent trading level; stock ranged ~600-950+ through H1 2026)
Schneider Electric Infrastructure.fy26_financials: Revenue ~Rs 29.1 billion (+10% YoY), Net income ~Rs 2.13 billion (down ~21% YoY on margin compression from higher expenses); Q3 FY26 revenue +21% YoY but net income -12% YoY
current_global_impact: Analyst narrative explicitly cites 'Grid Modernization And Data Center Demand' as the long-term upside driver for the stock

Revenue growth has been strong (+10-21% across recent quarters) but profit margins have compressed due to higher input/operating costs, showing the data-centre-driven volume growth hasn't yet translated fully to the bottom line
Stock has been volatile - down ~16% over 1 year (as of mid-Jan 2026 readings) even as revenue grew, before recovering later in H1 2026
markets_served: Parent Schneider Electric SE serves global data-centre/hyperscaler customers directly outside the India listed entity
expected_future_impact: Revenue is forecast by analysts to grow ~26-29% p.a. over the next 2-3 years, ahead of the broader Indian electrical industry's ~18-20% forecast growth, largely on grid-modernization and data-centre demand
Margin recovery (from the recent ~7-9% profit margin back toward historical ~10%+ levels) is the key swing factor for further re-rating

Blue Star.ai_link: Major Indian air-conditioning and commercial refrigeration company expanding into data-centre precision and liquid cooling - critical thermal-management infrastructure for high-density AI server racks (120-150kW/rack vs 10-15kW for legacy servers).
current_global_impact: Expanding into the data-centre sector with a focus on high-margin liquid-cooling solutions; management views data centres, factories and buildings as more profitable segments than traditional infrastructure projects
Developing in-house liquid-cooling models expected to be ready within ~12 months (as of early-2026 disclosures) and is seeking international technology partnerships for advanced cooling solutions

Net profit up sharply for 2 consecutive quarters (Rs 80.66 crore to Rs 227.05 crore, ~64.5% average quarterly growth) and revenue up for 3 consecutive quarters (Rs 2.43K crore to Rs 4.11K crore, ~22.9% average quarterly growth)
Positions itself as a market leader in MEP (mechanical, electrical, plumbing) and EPC execution specifically for data centres
markets_served: Primarily India; capability-building for future global-standard liquid-cooling deployments
expected_future_impact: Because Blue Star's liquid-cooling capability is still ~12 months from launch (as of early 2026), it remains a 'follower' relative to global pure-plays like Vertiv, but its scale in India's cooling/MEP market gives it a strong distribution advantage once ready

India's data-centre cooling investment opportunity has been estimated at roughly Rs 23,500 crore by market research cited in trade press, a large addressable market if Blue Star executes on its roadmap

Voltas.ai_link: Tata Group air-conditioning and MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing) major entering the data-centre cooling segment via its MEP vertical, supplying energy-efficient chillers for data-centre thermal management.
current_global_impact: Stock market cap up ~11.77% over 1 year to ~Rs 50,000 crore (as of April 2026); 1-year total price return of ~100.9%, with a TTM P/E of ~89.92 reflecting rich valuation
Leveraging its extensive MEP vertical to enter data centres, viewed internally as attractive, fast-track projects with quicker profitability than traditional infrastructure work

Enhanced its chiller offering (energy-efficient screw and centrifugal chillers, including oil-free variants) through a new technology partner, directly addressing data-centre operators' high energy-expenditure concerns
markets_served: Primarily India (MEP/EPC execution for data centres, commercial and residential cooling)
expected_future_impact: Unlike Blue Star, Voltas does not yet have liquid cooling on its roadmap, focusing instead on advanced air-side (chiller) solutions - a narrower but nearer-term data-centre cooling opportunity

As one of three major listed HVAC players (with KRN Heat Exchanger and Blue Star) pursuing the same data-centre cooling opportunity, execution speed and technology partnerships will determine relative market-share gains

Astra Microwave Products.ai_link: Primarily a defence/aerospace RF and microwave systems company (radars, AESA antennas, telemetry); AI/semiconductor relevance is indirect, via its Aelius Semiconductors Pte Ltd subsidiary and a board-approved demerger of its Space, Meteorology & Hydrology business into a separate entity (ASTPL, approved 10Jun26) - not a core AI-infrastructure play.
current_global_impact: Stock up ~72% over 1 year and ~79.7% over 6 months (as of 07Jul26), driven primarily by defence-sector order momentum (e.g. Rs 135 crore DRDO radar-upgrade order) rather than AI-specific demand
Has supported India's space programme and contributed to 35 radars, 25 satellites and ~15 strategic programmes to date
Board approved a scheme of arrangement to demerge its Space, Meteorology & Hydrology business into a separate listed entity (ASTPL) on a 1:1 share-entitlement basis (June 2026)
expected_future_impact: Its Aelius Semiconductors subsidiary gives some optionality in the broader India semiconductor ecosystem, but this is a small, early-stage part of the business relative to its core defence/RF electronics revenue

Included here for completeness given its semiconductor subsidiary and electronics manufacturing base, but investors should treat its AI/data-centre linkage as materially weaker than pure-plays like Netweb, STL or HFCL

Data Patterns (India).category: Defence & Aerospace Electronics (vertically integrated chip/board design)
Data Patterns (India).ai_link: Vertically integrated defence and aerospace electronics solutions provider (radar processors, avionics, electronic warfare, automatic test equipment); relevance to the AI/semiconductor theme is indirect, stemming from in-house PCB/electronic-system design capability and its own internal Gen-AI adoption for IT modernization - not an AI-infrastructure product company.
Data Patterns (India).fy26_financials: Revenue Rs 924.77 crore (+30.55% YoY), Consolidated net profit Rs 271.37 crore (+22.34% YoY); Q4 FY26 PAT Rs 138.38 crore (+21.3% YoY) despite a 12.96% YoY dip in Q4 revenue
current_global_impact: FY26 order inflows rose 216% YoY to Rs 1,121 crore, reflecting strong defence-sector order momentum
Launched the Mission Management and Display Computer (MMDC) for India's LCA Tejas Mk2 fighter jet and its avionics network during FY26

Internally adopted Gen-AI for search/productivity and cloud-based platforms as part of its own IT modernization - illustrative of AI adoption as an end-user rather than as an AI-infrastructure supplier
Named alongside Astra Microwave and Bharat Dynamics in market commentary on broader defence-sector catalysts (e.g. Sudarshan Chakra project, BrahMos ecosystem)
expected_future_impact: Continued strong order-inflow growth (+216% YoY in FY26) supports revenue visibility, though this is defence-programme-driven rather than AI/data-centre-driven

Included here for completeness given its electronics/chip-design vertical integration, but its AI-sector linkage is considerably more indirect than server, connectivity or power-infrastructure names in this basket

drivers: Global hyperscaler AI capex reset sharply higher - reports cite 2026 hyperscaler AI capital spending raised to ~$750 billion (from ~$670bn), expected to cross $1 trillion in 2027, directly driving global demand for servers, optical connectivity, transformers, switchgear and cooling that Indian suppliers are winning orders for
India's own data-centre IT load has grown from ~350MW (2019) to ~1.5-1.6GW (2025), a ~29% CAGR, creating a large domestic demand base alongside export orders
India-US trade deal (tariffs cut from 50% to 18% on Indian goods, reported ~Feb 2026) seen as a positive catalyst for India's electronics/semiconductor/transformer export ecosystem
IndiaAI Mission and government semiconductor/electronics manufacturing incentives (reports cite a ~Rs 1.9 lakh crore package) are supporting domestic AI-hardware and EMS capacity build-out

Chronic global shortage/lead-time in fibre 'preform' manufacturing (60-70% of optical fibre cost, concentrated in China) is a structural tailwind for Indian fibre players investing in backward integration (STL, HFCL)
positive_news: Sterlite Technologies: >$1 billion multi-year hyperscaler AI data-centre optical connectivity order
HFCL: ~Rs 10,159 crore international hyperscaler order plus a ~Rs 496 crore AI data-centre export order; order book has crossed Rs 21,000 crore
Netweb Technologies: Rs 1,734 crore Sovereign AI/IndiaAI Mission order using NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs; new Tyrone GB200 AI supercomputer launch
CG Power: Rs 900 crore US data-centre power transformer order
ABB India: record Rs 11,094 crore order backlog with data centres now ~11% of the book; $75 million India capex commitment including domestic data-centre automation capability

Blue Star & Voltas: both Tata/Tata-peer-scale HVAC majors formally entering the data-centre cooling segment, with Blue Star developing in-house liquid cooling and Voltas rolling out oil-free chillers via a new technology partner
Syrma SGS Technology: new PCB manufacturing facility in Andhra Pradesh and a new EMS JV with Kaga Electronics, alongside a ~99% one-year stock rally
Data Patterns: FY26 order inflows up 216% YoY to Rs 1,121 crore (though defence-driven, not AI-specific)
negative_news: Kaynes Technology and Dixon Technologies both saw sharp stock corrections (down ~43% and ~38-42% from respective 52-week highs) on weak quarterly results/regulatory delays, showing that AI/data-centre optionality alone hasn't insulated EMS names from execution risk
Voltamp Transformers' Q4 FY26 profit fell ~50% YoY on copper/aluminium input cost pressure, a sector-wide margin risk for transformer makers
Tejas Networks posted rising losses in Q4 FY26 despite revenue growth, with shares falling over 5% on the print - a reminder that order/product-launch news doesn't always translate to profitability

Schneider Electric Infrastructure's net income fell ~12-21% YoY in recent quarters even as revenue grew, showing margin compression despite the 'grid modernization and data centre demand' growth narrative
Several of the best-performing stocks in this basket (STL, HFCL, Syrma SGS) have already rallied 200-450%+ over 6-12 months, raising valuation and 'priced-in expectations' risk
sector_level_summary.current_global_impact_overview: Indian AI-infrastructure-adjacent companies are increasingly winning direct export orders from global hyperscalers (US/Europe) for optical connectivity and power transformers, and are now also building dedicated capability for data-centre power distribution (ABB) and liquid/precision cooling (Blue Star, Voltas) - marking a shift from purely domestic telecom/power-capex plays to genuine participants across the full global AI data-centre supply chain (compute, connectivity, power and cooling).
sector_level_summary.expected_future_impact_overview: With global hyperscaler capex guided toward $1 trillion+ by 2027 and India's own data-centre capacity scaling rapidly, order flow for optical fibre, power transformers/switchgear, AI servers, data-centre cooling and EMS-manufactured hardware is expected to keep growing - but recent sharp re-ratings (some stocks up 200-450% in a year) mean execution delivery, not just order announcements, will increasingly determine further stock performance. Cooling (Blue Star, Voltas) is the least mature of the four sub-themes covered here (compute, connectivity, power, cooling), with liquid-cooling capability still ~12 months from launch as of early 2026.
sector_level_summary.risk_level: High - most names in this basket are small/mid-cap, high-beta, and richly valued after very large recent rallies; order-win headlines can move stocks 5-20% in a single session. ABB India and Voltas/Blue Star are comparatively larger, more diversified names with somewhat lower single-stock risk than pure-plays like STL, HFCL or Netweb
sector_level_summary.momentum: Strong positive momentum in optical connectivity/fibre names (STL, HFCL), AI-server pure-plays (Netweb), EMS (Syrma SGS) and power/electrification (ABB India); weaker/negative momentum recently in some EMS names (Kaynes, Dixon) and in Tejas Networks after earnings disappointments
sector_level_summary.sentiment: Bullish on the structural AI/data-centre capex theme, but increasingly stock-specific and valuation-sensitive after the sharp 2026 rally in several names; cooling and defence-adjacent names (Blue Star, Voltas, Astra Microwave, Data Patterns) carry more indirect/diluted AI exposure than the pure-play compute and connectivity names
key_global_markets_catered_to: USA (hyperscaler data-centre orders - STL, HFCL, CG Power)
Europe/UK (STL's fibre/connectivity partnerships with BT, Netomnia, Connexin)
APAC/MEA (STL's Vocus, du Telecom partnerships; Tejas Networks' exports to ~75 countries)
Domestic India (IndiaAI Mission, BharatNet, government/defence AI compute - Netweb, HFCL, ABB India's data-centre automation capex, Blue Star/Voltas cooling projects)
